name: Camino Sanabrés: Olleros de Tera Loop
name_original: La Presa de Agavanzal desde Olleros de Tera — ida y vuelta
description: Hike the moderate 5.4-mile Camino Sanabrés: Olleros de Tera Loop, offering scenic views of the Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir.
region_intro: Hiking around Calzadilla De Tera offers routes through a landscape characterized by gentle terrain, often following river courses and passing through forested areas. The region features low elevation gains, making many trails accessible for various fitness levels. Ancient trees and historical sites are present along some paths, providing points of interest. Best hiking trails around Calzadilla De Tera The most popular hiking route is Bosque Las Majadas y arboles milenarios — vuelta desde San Pedro de Ceque, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through ancient woodlands. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castros de Castillan y Valdemera — circular desde Ferreras de Arriba, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.4 km) path. This circular trail explores the area's historical settlements and gentle hills. Local hikers also love the Camarzana de Tera loop from Camarzana de Tera, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through open countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking around Calzadilla De Tera is defined by river valleys, ancient forests, and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to moderate excursions. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 8 hikers have used komoot to explore Calzadilla De Tera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect in Calzadilla De Tera?
A: Hiking in Calzadilla De Tera primarily features routes through gentle terrain, often following river courses and passing through forested areas. The trails generally have low elevation gains, making them accessible for various fitness levels. You'll find a mix of ancient woodlands, historical sites, and open countryside.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Calzadilla De Tera?
A: Yes, Calzadilla De Tera offers easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24310588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camarzana de Tera loop from Camarzana de Tera&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.0-mile (6.4 km) path through open countryside, typically completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes.

Q: What are some longer hiking options in the Calzadilla De Tera area?
A: For a longer experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022496865/bosque-las-majadas-y-arboles-milenarios-vuelta-desde-san-pedro-de-ceque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque Las Majadas y arboles milenarios — vuelta desde San Pedro de Ceque&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route spans 7.5 miles (12.0 km) and takes approximately 3 hours 6 minutes, leading you through ancient woodlands.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many trails in the region are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025181993/castros-de-castillan-y-valdemera-circular-desde-ferreras-de-arriba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castros de Castillan y Valdemera — circular desde Ferreras de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.2-mile (8.4 km) path that explores historical settlements and gentle hills. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305488286/camino-sanabres-olleros-de-tera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés: Olleros de Tera Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in history. You can explore historical settlements along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025181993/castros-de-castillan-y-valdemera-circular-desde-ferreras-de-arriba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castros de Castillan y Valdemera — circular desde Ferreras de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3941370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Pilgrim of Rionegro del Puente&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some trails follow river courses. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity, offering scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24310776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area by the River loop from Calzadilla de Tera&lt;/a&gt; is another route that highlights the area's connection to its waterways.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Calzadilla De Tera?
A: Given the gentle terrain and forested areas, spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking in Calzadilla De Tera, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along river paths, but it's advisable to start early to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any facilities or picnic spots along the trails?
A: Yes, there are facilities available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3858806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area by the River&lt;/a&gt; is a designated spot that can be accessed via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24310776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area by the River loop from Calzadilla de Tera&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a break during your hike.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Calzadilla De Tera region?
A: There are at least 6 distinct hiking trails documented for the Calzadilla De Tera region, ranging from easy to moderate difficulty, ensuring options for various preferences.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Calzadilla De Tera?
A: Hikers often appreciate the region's tranquil river valleys and ancient forests. The trails are known for their gentle elevation changes, making them enjoyable for a relaxed outdoor experience. The presence of historical sites also adds an interesting cultural dimension to the walks.

Q: Can I find trails that lead through ancient woodlands?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022496865/bosque-las-majadas-y-arboles-milenarios-vuelta-desde-san-pedro-de-ceque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque Las Majadas y arboles milenarios — vuelta desde San Pedro de Ceque&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to take you through ancient woodlands, offering a unique natural experience.
tips: Beautiful hiking route along the Tera River towards the Agavanzal reservoir. The Tera Valley is an incredibly important natural landscape.
The reservoir of Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal is located in the municipality of Olleros de Tera. It dams the waters of the Tera River.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Camino Sanabrés: Olleros de Tera Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop typically takes around 2 hours and 20 minutes to complete. This estimate is for continuous hiking and does not include stops for breaks or enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Camino Sanabrés: Olleros de Tera Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate. While it has a relatively low elevation gain of about 45 meters, a moderate rating suggests there might be some inclines or uneven terrain, requiring a reasonable level of fitness. Beginners with some hiking experience should be able to manage it.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail offers picturesque views of the Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir, formed by the damming of the Tera River. You'll experience views of the expansive reservoir from one side of the dam and the river valley below from the other. The broader area transitions from gentler river walking to a landscape shaped by the reservoir.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the primary notable feature is the scenic Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir, which offers beautiful views. You will also follow sections of the Tera River Trail, showcasing the interaction of the river with the local landscape.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Olleros de Tera Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact loop are not provided, the trail starts and ends in Olleros de Tera. Hikers typically find parking within the village or near the trailhead, which is often accessible from local roads.

faqs.question: Is the Camino Sanabrés: Olleros de Tera Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near wildlife, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Camino Sanabrés: Olleros de Tera Loop. It is a publicly accessible hiking route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this loop is generally during spring, early summer, and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring colder weather and potentially less accessible paths.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route covers significant sections of the larger Camino Sanabrés (East) (96% of the route) and the Camino Sanabrés (72% of the route). It also overlaps with the Mtb Camino Sanabrés (Este) / Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Leste) for 72% of its length.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead in Olleros de Tera?
faqs.answer: Olleros de Tera is a small village, and while specific amenities like cafes or pubs directly at the trailhead are not guaranteed, you may find local services within the village itself. It's advisable to bring your own water and snacks.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this loop?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, bring comfortable hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing for the weather conditions. A basic first-aid kit is also recommended.
